channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A second wind-whipped wildfire is burning out of control in the Los Angeles area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A fast-moving wildfire broke out Tuesday in the inland foothills northeast of Los Angeles hours after another blaze tore through the city’s Pacific Palisades neighborhood along the coast, destroying many homes and prompting evacuation orders for tens of thousands.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, CHRISTOPHER WEBER and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CNN defamation trial comes at a rough time for legacy media
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — At a particularly inopportune time for legacy media and CNN, the news outlet is on trial in Florida this week, accused of defaming a Navy veteran involved in rescuing endangered Afghans from that country when the U.S. ended its involvement there in 2021.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Peter Yarrow of folk-music trio Peter, Paul and Mary dies at 86
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Peter Yarrow, the singer-songwriter best known as one-third of Peter, Paul and Mary, the folk-music trio whose impassioned harmonies transfixed millions as they lifted their voices in favor of civil rights and against war, has died. He was 86.
item.dc:creator: JOHN ROGERS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Southern US shivers as new winter storm threatens snow for Texas, Oklahoma and Arkansas
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — A developing winter storm threatens to drop snow, sleet and freezing rain on parts of Texas, Oklahoma and Arkansas as frigid air that escaped the Arctic plunges temperatures to subfreezing levels in some of the southernmost points of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hostages in Gaza endure another winter as their families plead for a ceasefire
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — When Luis Har was kidnapped by Hamas-led militants on the warm morning of Oct. 7, 2023, he was forced into Gaza wearing shorts and a T-shirt.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jimmy Carter continues to lie in state at Capitol Rotunda ahead of his state funeral
item.description: Former U.S. president Jimmy Carter will continue to lie in state after his remains arrived in Washington a day earlier as part of state funeral rites.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A second wind-whipped wildfire is burning out of control in the Los Angeles area
item.description: A fast-moving wildfire has broken out in the inland foothills northeast of Los Angeles hours after another blaze tore through the city’s Pacific Palisades neighborhood along the coast, destroying many homes and prompting evacuation orders for tens of thousands.
item.dc:creator: Jaimie Ding, Christopher Weber And Julie Watson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Colorado governor walks a tightrope in approaching Trump. Deportations likely to be the first test
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Colorado Gov. Jared Polis donned safety glasses and seized the handle of the electric saw, guiding the buzzing blade down through a stack of printed-out executive orders dating back decades.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: California governor says Pacific Palisades wildfire has destroyed many structures as winds kick up
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A wildfire whipped up by extreme winds swept through a Los Angeles hillside dotted with celebrity residences Tuesday, burning homes in Pacific Palisades and prompting evacuation orders for tens of thousands.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JANIE HAR and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Second Los Angeles-area fire breaks out as firefighters battle raging Palisades inferno
item.description: California brush fires struck on Tuesday, with a second fire breaking out in Pasadena, forcing some residents to flee by foot as evacuation routes are stunted highway closures.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris and others as 39th president returns to Washington
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington on Tuesday for state funeral rites that featured the kind of bipartisan praise and ceremonial pomp the Georgia Democrat rarely enjoyed at his political peak.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, JONATHAN J. COOPER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris and others as 39th president returns to Washington
item.description: Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington for three days of state funeral rites starting Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wildfire burns through LA enclave known for its beaches and celebrity allure
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Rimmed to the south by a 3-mile stretch of Pacific beach, and to the north by craggy bluffs and deep, verdant canyons, the seaside enclave of Pacific Palisades has long been a celebrity hideaway and magnet for those drawn to nature’s doorstep not far from the skyline of downtown L
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cybertruck driver used ChatGPT to plan Las Vegas attack, police say
item.description: Police found a 6-page manifesto on Matthew Livelsberger’s phone and say he used ChatGPT to plan his New Year's Day bombing at the front of the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas, the LVMPD said at a news conference Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, WGN 9

item.title: The polar vortex brings its bitter cold to the Southern US
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — The plunging polar vortex brought subfreezing temperatures Tuesday to some of the southernmost points of the U.S., threatening to dump snow on parts of Texas and Oklahoma in coming days and contributing to a power outage in Virginia's capital that made the water unsafe to drink.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Driver in Kansas City viral video explains why she jumped out of car
item.description: The Kansas City woman seen in a viral video where she ditched her car while it slid on black ice recounts what happened, and how the frightening moment led to a heartwarming ending
item.dc:creator: Regan Porter, KRON4

item.title: Palisades Fire in Los Angeles scorches 2,921 acres; many homes destroyed
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - Tens of thousands of residents in the western Los Angeles area were under mandatory evacuation orders Tuesday evening as a wind-driven wildfire exploded in size and swept through communities in the Pacific Palisades. The Palisades Fire was first reported around 10:30 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: New York appeals court denies halting Trump's sentencing
item.description: A New York appellate judge on Tuesday declined to suspend President-elect Trump’s Friday sentencing on his hush money criminal conviction.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man accused of setting woman on fire on Brooklyn subway pleads not guilty
item.description: Sebastian Zapeta has been accused of intentionally setting fire to 57-year-old Debrina Kawam of New Jersey at an NYC subway station on Dec. 22.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump refuses to rule out use of military force to take control of Greenland, Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday said he would not rule out the use of military force to seize control of the Panama Canal and Greenland, as he declared U.S. control of both to be vital to American national security.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 found dead in compartment of JetBlue plane at Florida airport
item.description: The two were found dead during a routine post-flight maintenance inspection on Monday, according to JetBlue.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, Fox 5

item.title: 3-year-old boy dies from Honolulu fireworks explosion injuries, raising toll to 4
item.description: The U.S. military flew six of the injured to Phoenix for treatment on Saturday because Hawaii’s lone burn unit doesn’t have enough capacity to care for all the victims.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Getty Images and Shutterstock to merge to become $3.7 billion visual content company
item.description: Visual content companies Shutterstock and Getty Images will join to become a $3.7 billion visual content company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rapper Trae tha Truth reunited with daughter after abduction
item.description: On New Year’s Day, Trae received the news he had been praying for: Truth was found safe near the Mexican border in California.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Uber and Lyft to offer driverless rideshare cars
item.description: Customers in Atlanta and Austin, Texas, will be able to hail one of these driverless rideshare cars via the Uber app.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Court proceedings for man charged in UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing are delayed several weeks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man accused of gunning down UnitedHealthcare's CEO outside a New York City hotel is unlikely to appear in Manhattan federal court again before mid-February after prosecutors and lawyers on Monday requested more time to prepare the case for trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rudy Giuliani is in contempt of court in $148 million defamation case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Rudy Giuliani was found in contempt of court Monday for failing to properly respond to requests for information as he turned over assets to satisfy a $148 million defamation judgment granted to two Georgia election workers. Judge Lewis J.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: First US bird flu death is announced in Louisiana
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The first U.S. bird flu death has been reported — a person in Louisiana who had been hospitalized with severe respiratory symptoms.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Gov. Kathy Hochul wants to triple New York's child tax credit
item.description: "The cost of living and raising a family is still too high," said Hochul. "We’re proposing a massive increase to put money back in the pockets of hardworking families.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Hush money judge refuses to delay Trump's criminal sentencing
item.description: The New York judge who oversaw President-elect Trump’s criminal trial last spring declined his request to pause Friday’s sentencing, setting up a last-minute appellate battle as he seeks to stave off the proceeding.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: McDonald's launching new value menu: Here are the options
item.description: McDonald’s will launch a new “McValue platform” on Tuesday, aiming to give customers “even more ways to enjoy the food they love, for less," the fast-food giant says. The McValue menu, first announced in November, will include nationwide, local and app-based offers.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Detachment risk prompts recall of 145K trailer hitch locks
item.description: The recall says the vehicle may become detached from whatever it's towing due to the potentially loose part on the hitch locks.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, ABC 4

item.title: Lions beat Vikings 31-9, win NFC North and No. 1 seed, dropping division rivals to No. 5
item.description: Jahmyr Gibbs was the game-breaking player the Detroit Lions needed to get their high-powered offense in gear — and give them a first-round bye and home-field advantage in the NFC playoffs.
item.dc:creator: LARRY LAGE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The heaviest snowfall in a decade is possible as a wintry blast roils parts of the US
item.description: A blast of snow, ice, wind and plunging temperatures stirred up dangerous travel conditions in parts of the central U.S. on Sunday, as a disruptive winter storm brought the possibility of the heaviest snowfall in a decade to some areas.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why you shouldn't charge your phone to 100%, according to experts
item.description: It may be tempting to charge to 100%, but experts say 80% may be enough — and beneficial for your phone. Here's why.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4
